narrative: OMX30 faces a challenging open after heavy overnight selloff across US equities (NASDAQ -4.18%) and Asian bourses (Nikkei -3.85%), driven by surging US 10Y yields (+132bp to 4.536%) and escalating Middle East tensions. While OMX remains structurally bullish (6.1% above 200-MA), the combination of negative correlation signal (-1.468%), weak ADX (0.7), and MACD below signal suggests tactical vulnerability. Oil's 4.07% surge will pressure industrials and consumer demand, while yield strength diverts capital from growth and compresses DCF models. Key risk: further escalation in Iran conflict or additional Fed hawkishness could trigger rapid de-risking.
morningBrief: OMX30 opening under pressure from overnight global selloff and geopolitical shock. Expect gap-down open with initial support at 3085 (lower Bollinger Band) and pivot 3123. ADX at 0.7 signals weak directional conviction — trade ranges until VIX stabilizes or fresh flows emerge. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) may find modest lift from 4.536% US yields, but tech/growth (Hexagon, Ericsson) vulnerable to NASDAQ sentiment (-4.18%) and DCF compression. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B) face dual headwinds: oil +4.07% raising input costs and USD/SEK +0.84% only partially offsetting margin pressure. Watch Boliden as copper +1.02% provides a bright spot. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET critical for yield trajectory — a weak print could stabilize OMX; a beat could reignite risk-off. Position size tight until volatility regime clarifies.
drivers: US yields spike +132bp to 4.536% compressing tech/DCF valuations
Geopolitical escalation (Tehran airstrikes, Israel-Iran tensions) spiking safe-haven demand
Oil surge +4.07% to $96.88 pressuring consumer spending and industrial input costs
Weak ADX (0.7) limits conviction; RSI 53.1 neutral — technical backdrop fragile

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield surge to 4.536% widens NIM expectations; however, geopolitical risk-off and deposit flight concerns may limit upside.
Oil +4.07% to $96.88 raises input/energy costs; USD/SEK strength (+0.84%) provides limited offset. Global demand uncertainty from equity selloff.
NASDAQ -4.18% overnight; US 10Y +132bp compresses DCF valuations. Weak ADX limits technical support.
Copper +1.02% and gold -0.44% mixed signals. Boliden upside capped by global recession fears; SSAB pressured by industrial weakness.
Yield spike to 4.536% raises refinancing and discount-rate costs. Risk-off sentiment pressures property valuations.
Oil spike pressures discretionary spending; weak EUR/SEK (-0.01%) increases H&M sourcing costs; global risk-off dampens consumer confidence.
Defensive rotation in risk-off environment; yields up typically neutral for pharma. Safe-haven demand supports relative outperformance.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.02% provides direct revenue upside; gold flat (-0.44%) limits full benefit. Monitor if industrial selloff accelerates.
Yield strength +132bp supportive, but geopolitical shock and deposit flight risks may offset NIM gains. Watch for market stabilization.
Oil cost headwinds; global industrial PMI weakness from equity selloff; USD/SEK strength insufficient to offset margin pressure.

watchList.reason: NASDAQ -4.18% overnight; DCF compression from 4.536% yields; tech sentiment weak. ADX too low for strong directional conviction.

keyLevels.technicalNote: Bollinger %B=0.37 (lower half) suggests limited upside cushion; width 5.71% implies moderate volatility backdrop.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (today) — critical for yield trajectory and risk sentiment. No major Swedish data releases. Monitor for any escalation in Middle East situation or Fed commentary.
topRisk: Further geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel spiral, NATO involvement) could spike VIX beyond 25 and trigger sharp de-risking across OMX30. Secondary risk: weak US jobless claims could reinforce recession fears and extend tech/growth selloff.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal (hist=-4.533) signals momentum loss
ADX 0.7 (very weak) reduces confidence to Low — apply 30% magnitude haircut per rule
Stochastic K=23.7 (oversold-leaning) may signal mean-reversion, but global backdrop too bearish
Bollinger %B=0.37 in lower half suggests limited immediate upside
